compiled_state.title: As a sprint orchestrator, I can validate high-confidence SC coverage in Phase 1 using haiku's citation-based check
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Insert the haiku-tier SC coverage gate sub-step into sprint SKILL.md Preplanning Gate, handling the COVERED → Phase 3 happy path and fail-open on error.
**Why**: Enables sprint to confirm clear-cut SC coverage before Phase 3, eliminating wasted execution cycles for obviously well-covered epics.
**Scope**:
- IN: New gate sub-step in sprint SKILL.md after existing Step 2a ambiguity check, haiku dispatch (SC list + children descriptions), COVERED → Phase 3 routing, parse failure → skip + warn (fail-open), 0-SC epic → trivially pass with warning, ORCHESTRATOR_RESUME idempotency
- OUT: Sonnet/opus escalation (S2b), REPLAN_TRIGGER routing (S2b), prompt file content (S1)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint Phase 1 invokes the haiku agent with the epic's SC list and children descriptions before Phase 3 batch preparation
  ← Satisfies: "Sprint Phase 1 runs a 3-tier SC coverage check before Phase 3 batch preparation"
- When this story is complete, when haiku cites all SCs as covered, sprint proceeds to Phase 3 without invoking preplanning
  ← Satisfies: "When all SCs are COVERED post-cascade, sprint proceeds normally to Phase 3"
- When this story is complete, parse failure on haiku output logs a warning and skips the gate; sprint proceeds to Phase 3 (fail-open)
- When this story is complete, epics with 0 SCs receive a warning log and the gate is skipped trivially
- When this story is complete, the gate is idempotent: if ORCHESTRATOR_RESUME indicates the gate already completed, the gate is skipped on resume
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Use a sub-step number (e.g., Step 2b or 2c) rather than renumbering existing steps — shifting step numbers breaks CHECKPOINT and ORCHESTRATOR_RESUME comment references throughout sprint SKILL.md
- [Performance] Haiku fast path minimizes latency for well-covered epics; only unclear cases escalate to sonnet (S2b)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
